title: commiphora campestris subsp. glabrata
intro: Commiphora campestris subsp. glabrata is a subspecies of the Commiphora plant, also known as African myrrh, which is a small tree or shrub native to East Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Commiphora campestris subsp. glabrata include wound healing, antiseptic properties, and the ability to reduce inflammation and pain. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
